title: CLI Reference
description: Creates a new GoSPA project with the specified name.
content: CLI Reference Complete reference for the GoSPA command-line interface tools. gospa create Creates a new GoSPA project with the specified name. Flag Short Default Description --module -m myapp Go module name for the project --output -o myapp Output directory for the project --no-git false Skip .gitignore creation --docker false Add a default Dockerfile gospa build Builds the application for production deployment. Flag Short Default Description --output -o dist Output directory for built files --platform -p runtime.GOOS Target platform (OS) --arch -a runtime.GOARCH Target architecture --env -e production Build environment --no-minify false Disable JavaScript minification --no-compress false Disable asset compression --no-static false Skip copying static assets --all false Build for all platforms gospa dev Starts the development server with hot reload. Flag Short Default Description --port -p 3000 Server port --host -H localhost Server host --routes -r ./routes Routes directory --components -c ./components Components directory gospa generate Generates TypeScript types and route definitions. Flag Short Default Description --input -i . Input directory for exploration --output -o ./generated Output directory for generated files gospa add Add plugins and features to your project. Feature Description tailwind Add Tailwind CSS support postcss Add PostCSS with Tailwind extensions image Add image optimization validation Add form validation (Valibot + Go validator) seo Add SEO optimization (sitemap, meta tags) auth Add authentication (OAuth2, JWT, OTP) gospa prune Remove unused state from state stores to prevent memory leaks. Flag Short Default Description --dry-run -d false Show what would be pruned without removing --older-than -o 1h Remove state older than duration --all -a false Remove all state (use with caution) gospa clean Remove build artifacts and generated files. gospa watch Build and watch for changes. Project Structure A newly created GoSPA project has the following structure: │ ├── layout.templ # Root layout │ └── page.templ # Home page ├── generated/ # Auto-generated TypeScript │ ├── routes.ts # Route definitions │ └── types.ts # TypeScript types └── static/ # Static assets`, "plaintext", "structure") }

title: DOM Bindings
description: How to connect your reactive state to the DOM using data attributes and programmatic APIs.
content: DOM Bindings How to connect your reactive state to the DOM using data attributes and programmatic APIs. DOM Attributes Reference Attribute Description data-bind State binding (key:type) data-model Two-way binding (key) data-on Event handler (event:action:args) data-gospa-component Component ID Binding Types Different binding types for various DOM updates: Type Description Example text textContent update data-bind="message:text" html innerHTML (sanitized) data-bind="content:html" style Inline style property data-bind="color:style:color" class Toggle class name data-bind="isActive:class:active" attr Any attribute data-bind="href:attr:href" prop DOM property data-bind="checked:prop:checked" Programmatic Bindings Bind runes to DOM elements manually for more complex scenarios. @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript — uses global GoSPA object const element = document.getElementById('count'); const textRune = new GoSPA.Rune('Hello'); // One-way bindings GoSPA.bindElement(element, textRune); GoSPA.bindElement(element, htmlRune, { type: 'html' }); GoSPA.bindElement(element, colorRune, { type: 'style', attribute: 'color' }); // Two-way binding GoSPA.bindTwoWay(inputElement, textRune);`, `// TypeScript — ES module imports import { bindElement, bindTwoWay } from '@gospa/client'; // One-way bindings bindElement(element, textRune); bindElement(element, htmlRune, { type: 'html' }); bindElement(element, colorRune, { type: 'style', attribute: 'color' }); // Two-way binding bindTwoWay(inputElement, textRune);`, "bindings.ts") bindElement() Creates a one-way binding from a rune to a DOM element. @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript const count = new GoSPA.Rune(0); const countEl = document.getElementById('count'); // Basic text binding GoSPA.bindElement(countEl, count); // Style binding const color = new GoSPA.Rune('red'); const boxEl = document.getElementById('box'); GoSPA.bindElement(boxEl, color, { type: 'style', attribute: 'backgroundColor' }); // Class binding const isActive = new GoSPA.Rune(true); const itemEl = document.getElementById('item'); GoSPA.bindElement(itemEl, isActive, { type: 'class', className: 'active' }); // Attribute binding const href = new GoSPA.Rune('/home'); const linkEl = document.getElementById('link'); GoSPA.bindElement(linkEl, href, { type: 'attr', attribute: 'href' });`, `// TypeScript import { bindElement, rune } from '@gospa/client'; const count = rune(0); // Basic text binding bindElement(document.getElementById('count'), count); // Style binding const color = rune('red'); bindElement(document.getElementById('box'), color, { type: 'style', attribute: 'backgroundColor' }); // Class binding const isActive = rune(true); bindElement(document.getElementById('item'), isActive, { type: 'class', className: 'active' }); // Attribute binding const href = rune('/home'); bindElement(document.getElementById('link'), href, { type: 'attr', attribute: 'href' });`, "bindelement.ts") bindTwoWay() Creates a two-way binding between an input element and a rune. @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript const name = new GoSPA.Rune(''); // Two-way binding with input const input = document.querySelector('input[name="name"]'); GoSPA.bindTwoWay(input, name); // Now name.get() reflects input value // And input.value updates when name.set() is called`, `// TypeScript import { bindTwoWay, rune } from '@gospa/client'; const name = rune(''); // Two-way binding with input const input = document.querySelector('input[name="name"]'); bindTwoWay(input, name); // Now name.get() reflects input value // And input.value updates when name.set() is called`, "bindtwoway.ts") Conditional Rendering Render elements conditionally based on rune state. @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript const isLoggedIn = new GoSPA.Rune(false); // Show element only when condition is true const adminPanel = document.getElementById('admin-panel'); GoSPA.renderIf(adminPanel, isLoggedIn); // With inverse condition const loginForm = document.getElementById('login-form'); GoSPA.renderIf(loginForm, isLoggedIn, { inverse: true });`, `// TypeScript import { renderIf, rune } from '@gospa/client'; const isLoggedIn = rune(false); // Show element only when condition is true renderIf(document.getElementById('admin-panel'), isLoggedIn); // With inverse condition renderIf(document.getElementById('login-form'), isLoggedIn, { inverse: true });`, "conditional.ts") List Rendering Render lists from reactive arrays with efficient DOM updates. @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript const todos = new GoSPA.Rune([ { id: 1, text: 'Learn GoSPA' }, { id: 2, text: 'Build an app' } ]); // Render list with key-based reconciliation GoSPA.renderList( document.getElementById('todo-list'), todos, { key: (todo) => todo.id, render: (todo, index) => { const li = document.createElement('li'); li.textContent = todo.text; return li; } } );`, `// TypeScript import { renderList, rune } from '@gospa/client'; interface Todo { id: number; text: string; } const todos = rune ([]); // Render list with key-based reconciliation renderList( document.getElementById('todo-list'), todos, { key: (todo) => todo.id, render: (todo, index) => { const li = document.createElement('li'); li.textContent = todo.text; return li; } } );`, "list.ts") Sanitization Configure HTML sanitization for safe innerHTML bindings. @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript // Set custom sanitizer GoSPA.setSanitizer((html) => { // Your sanitization logic return sanitizedHtml; }); // Get current sanitizer const sanitize = GoSPA.getSanitizer();`, `// TypeScript import { setSanitizer, getSanitizer } from '@gospa/client'; // Set custom sanitizer setSanitizer((html: string) => { // Your sanitization logic return sanitizedHtml; }); // Get current sanitizer const sanitize = getSanitizer();`, "sanitization.ts") Binding Registry Internal binding management for cleanup and debugging. @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript // Register a custom binding const bindingId = GoSPA.registerBinding(element, rune, config); // Unregister when cleaning up GoSPA.unregisterBinding(bindingId);`, `// TypeScript import { registerBinding, unregisterBinding } from '@gospa/client'; // Register a custom binding const bindingId = registerBinding(element, rune, config); // Unregister when cleaning up unregisterBinding(bindingId);`, "registry.ts") Previous Overview Next Navigation & Events }

title: Navigation & Events
description: Managing SPA navigation and advanced event handling.
content: Navigation & Events Managing SPA navigation and advanced event handling. Navigation API Programmatic navigation for SPA behavior. ; // With options await navigate('/dashboard', { replace: true, // Replace history entry scrollToTop: true // Scroll to top after navigation }); // History navigation back(); // Go back one page forward(); // Go forward one page go(-2); // Go back 2 pages go(3); // Go forward 3 pages`, "typescript", "navigation.ts") Prefetching Preload pages for instant navigation. ; // Prefetch multiple pages prefetch(['/blog/post-1', '/blog/post-2']); // Prefetch all links matching a selector prefetchLinks('a[data-prefetch]');`, "typescript", "prefetch.ts") Navigation State Track and manage navigation state reactively. ; // Reactive properties nav.currentPath; // Current URL path nav.isNavigating; // True during navigation nav.historyLength; // Number of history entries // Navigate with state navigate('/dashboard', { state: { from: 'home' } }); // Access state const state = nav.getState();`, "typescript", "nav-state.ts") Lifecycle Callbacks Register callbacks that run before or after navigation. const unsubBefore = onBeforeNavigate((path) => { console.log('Starting navigation to:', path); // Return false to cancel if (path === '/admin' && !isLoggedIn) { return false; } }); // After navigation const unsubAfter = onAfterNavigate((path) => { console.log('Finished navigation to:', path); // Analytics, scroll restoration, etc. }); // Cleanup unsubBefore(); unsubAfter();`, "typescript", "lifecycle.ts") Global DOM Events The runtime dispatches custom events on the document. Event Detail Description gospa:navigated {"{"}path, state{"}"} After successful navigation gospa:navigation-start {"{"}from, to{"}"} Before navigation starts gospa:navigation-error {"{"}error, path{"}"} Navigation failed => { console.log('Navigated to:', e.detail.path); // Re-run third-party initializers here });`, "javascript", "script.js") Event Handling Advanced event handling with modifiers and delegation. => { console.log('Form submitted'); }); // Available modifiers: // :prevent - preventDefault() // :stop - stopPropagation() // :once - Run once then remove // :capture - Capture phase // :passive - Passive listener // Event delegation delegate(document.body, '.item', 'click', (e, target) => { console.log('Item clicked:', target); }); // Debounced handler const debouncedClick = debounce((e) => { console.log('Debounced click'); }, 300); button.addEventListener('click', debouncedClick); // Throttled handler const throttledScroll = throttle((e) => { console.log('Scroll position:', window.scrollY); }, 100); window.addEventListener('scroll', throttledScroll);`, "typescript", "events.ts") Event Modifiers Chain modifiers for complex event handling. => { // preventDefault() and stopPropagation() called navigate('/new-page'); }); // One-time handler with prevent on(modal, 'click:once:prevent', (e) => { closeModal(); });`, "typescript", "modifiers.ts") Keyboard Events Keyboard shortcuts and key combinations. => { closeModal(); }); // Key combination onKey(document, 'Ctrl+s', (e) => { e.preventDefault(); saveDocument(); }); // Using keys constants onKey(document, keys.ESCAPE, () => closeModal()); onKey(document, keys.ENTER, () => submitForm()); // Multiple keys onKey(document, ['ArrowUp', 'ArrowDown'], (e) => { navigateList(e.key === 'ArrowUp' ? -1 : 1); });`, "typescript", "keyboard.ts") Manual Initialization Set up navigation manually for custom scenarios. ; // Cleanup when done destroyNavigation();`, "typescript", "init.ts") Navigation Options Configure navigation behavior at runtime via data attributes or JavaScript. Option Default Description data-gospa-progress true Show/hide the progress bar during navigation (set to "false" to disable) data-gospa-prefetch Enabled Prefetch links on hover/viewport entry (set to "false" to disable) data-gospa-view-transition Enabled Enable native View Transitions with smooth fades (set to "false" to disable) These options can be set on individual links via data attributes, or globally via the initNavigation() function. ;`, "html", "navigation-options.html") Previous DOM Bindings Next Transitions }

title: Runtime Overview
description: Complete reference for initializing and configuring the GoSPA client-side runtime.
content: Runtime Overview Complete reference for initializing and configuring the GoSPA client-side runtime. Installation The runtime is automatically injected into your pages by the GoSPA server. No manual installation required for the default runtime. For ES modules, install the client package: @components.DualCodeBlock( ` const count = new GoSPA.Rune(0); console.log(count.get()); `, `# TypeScript / ES Modules npm install @gospa/client # Import from the client { Rune, Effect, navigate } from '@gospa/client';`, "setup") Runtime Variants Variant Size Includes runtime.js ~17KB Full runtime + DOMPurify sanitizer runtime-simple.js ~11KB Lightweight runtime (no sanitizer) Rune Core reactive state container. Similar to Svelte's $state rune. @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript — uses global GoSPA object const count = new GoSPA.Rune(0); const name = GoSPA.rune('initial'); // factory function // Read console.log(count.get()); // 0 console.log(count.peek()); // 0 (without tracking) // Write count.set(2); count.update(n => n + 1); // Subscribe const unsubscribe = count.subscribe((value, oldValue) => { console.log('Changed:', oldValue, '->', value); }); // Cleanup unsubscribe();`, `// TypeScript — ES module imports import { Rune, rune } from '@gospa/client'; // Create const count = new Rune(0); const name = rune('initial'); // factory function // Read console.log(count.value); // 0 console.log(count.get()); // 0 console.log(count.peek()); // 0 (without tracking) // Write count.value = 1; count.set(2); count.update(n => n + 1); // Subscribe const unsubscribe = count.subscribe((value, oldValue) => { console.log('Changed:', oldValue, '->', value); }); // Cleanup unsubscribe();`, "rune.ts") Method Description get() Get value (tracks dependencies) set(value) Set new value update(fn) Update using function subscribe(fn) Subscribe to changes peek() Get value without tracking ID() Unique internal identifier Derived Computed reactive value that recalculates when dependencies change. @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript const count = new GoSPA.Rune(5); const doubled = new GoSPA.Derived(() => count.get() * 2); console.log(doubled.get()); // 10 count.set(10); console.log(doubled.get()); // 20 — automatically updated`, `// TypeScript import { Rune, Derived, derived } from '@gospa/client'; const count = new Rune(5); const doubled = new Derived(() => count.get() * 2); const summary = derived(() => 'Count: ' + count.get()); // Read console.log(doubled.value); // 10 // Subscribe doubled.subscribe(v => console.log('Doubled:', v)); // Cleanup doubled.dispose();`, "derived.ts") Effect Side effects that re-run when dependencies change. @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript const count = new GoSPA.Rune(0); // Create effect const myEffect = new GoSPA.Effect(() => { console.log('Count changed:', count.get()); // Optional cleanup function return () => { console.log('Cleanup before next run'); }; }); // Control myEffect.pause(); // Stop reacting myEffect.resume(); // Resume reacting myEffect.dispose(); // Permanently cleanup`, `// TypeScript import { Effect, effect } from '@gospa/client'; const count = new Rune(0); // Create effect const myEffect = new Effect(() => { console.log('Count changed:', count.get()); // Optional cleanup function return () => { console.log('Cleanup before next run'); }; }); // Control myEffect.pause(); // Stop reacting myEffect.resume(); // Resume reacting myEffect.dispose(); // Permanently cleanup`, "effect.ts") StateMap Collection of named runes for managing multiple state values. ; // Set (creates or updates) states.set('count', 0); states.set('name', 'GoSPA'); // Get const countRune = states.get ('count'); // Check states.has('count'); // true // Delete states.delete('name'); // Serialize const json = states.toJSON(); // {"{"}"count": 0{"}"} // Deserialize states.fromJSON({"{"}"count": 5, "name": "Restored"{"}"}); // Clear all states.clear();`, "typescript", "statemap.ts") Resource Async data fetching with loading/error states. => { const res = await fetch('/api/user'); return res.json(); }); // Check status console.log(userResource.status); // 'idle' | 'pending' | 'success' | 'error' console.log(userResource.isPending); // boolean console.log(userResource.data); // T | undefined console.log(userResource.error); // E | undefined // Fetch/refetch await userResource.refetch(); // Reset to idle userResource.reset();`, "typescript", "resource.ts") Utility Functions => { a.set(10); b.set(20); }); // Single notification after all changes // Execute without tracking untrack(() => { console.log(tracked.get()); // Not tracked }); // Watch for changes const unsub = watch(count, (value, oldValue) => { console.log('Changed:', oldValue, '->', value); }); // Debug helper (dev only) inspect(count); inspect(count).with((type, value) => { if (type === 'update') console.log('Updated:', value); });`, "typescript", "utilities.ts") Initialization ;`, "typescript", "init.ts") Global API The runtime exposes a global __GOSPA__ object for debugging. Previous Client Runtime Next DOM Bindings }

title: Transitions
description: Animate elements as they enter or leave the DOM with built-in or custom transitions.
content: Transitions Animate elements as they enter or leave the DOM with built-in or custom transitions. Built-in Transitions GoSPA provides several built-in transition functions. fade Smoothly transitions opacity from 0 to 1. ;`, "typescript", "fade.ts") fly Animates position (x, y) and opacity. ;`, "typescript", "fly.ts") slide Slides the element vertically. ;`, "typescript", "slide.ts") scale Scales the element from a starting point. ;`, "typescript", "scale.ts") blur Blurs the element in/out. ;`, "typescript", "blur.ts") crossfade Crossfade between elements. ;`, "typescript", "crossfade.ts") Easing Functions Control the timing curve of transitions with easing functions. ;`, "typescript", "easing.ts") linear Constant speed cubicOut Fast start, slow end cubicInOut Slow start/end, fast middle elasticOut Elastic overshoot bounceOut Bouncing effect Attribute API Use HTML attributes to declaratively apply transitions. Programmatic API Control transitions programmatically with JavaScript. transitionIn / transitionOut ; // Exit transition with callback transitionOut(element, fly, { x: -100, duration: 300 }, () => { element.remove(); });`, "typescript", "programmatic.ts") setupTransitions Auto-setup transitions from DOM attributes. ; // Setup on specific root setupTransitions(document.getElementById('app'));`, "typescript", "setup.ts") Transition Parameters Common parameters for all transition types. Parameter Type Default Description delay number 0 Delay before transition starts (ms) duration number 400 Transition duration (ms) easing function cubicOut Easing function opacity number 0 Starting opacity (fly, scale, blur) x number 0 Horizontal offset (fly) y number 0 Vertical offset (fly, slide) start number 0 Starting scale (scale) amount number 5 Blur amount in pixels (blur) Custom Transitions Create custom transition functions. { return { delay: 0, duration, easing: cubicOut, css: (t) => { const x = Math.sin(t * Math.PI * 4) * intensity * (1 - t); return 'transform: translateX(' + x + 'px)'; } }; } // Use custom transition transitionIn(element, wiggle, { intensity: 20 });`, "typescript", "custom.ts") Previous WebSocket Next Go API }

title: WebSocket Client
description: Real-time state synchronization between server and client.
content: WebSocket Client Real-time state synchronization between server and client. WSClient WebSocket client for real-time state synchronization with auto-reconnect and heartbeat support. reconnectInterval: 1000, // Base interval (default: 1000ms) maxReconnectAttempts: 10, // Max attempts (default: 10) heartbeatInterval: 30000, // Ping interval (default: 30s) onOpen: () => console.log('Connected'), onClose: (e) => console.log('Disconnected'), onError: (e) => console.error('Error'), onMessage: (msg) => console.log('Message:', msg) }); // Connect await ws.connect(); // Check state console.log(ws.state); // 'connecting' | 'connected' | 'disconnecting' | 'disconnected' console.log(ws.isConnected); // boolean // Send message ws.send({ type: 'update', payload: { key: 'value' } }); // Send with response const response = await ws.sendWithResponse({ type: 'init', componentId: 'my-component' }); // Request state sync ws.requestSync(); // Send action ws.sendAction('increment', { amount: 1 }); // Disconnect ws.disconnect(); // Get singleton instance const client = getWebSocketClient();`, "typescript", "wsclient.ts") Configuration Options Option Type Default Description url string - WebSocket server URL reconnect boolean true Auto-reconnect on disconnect reconnectInterval number 1000 Base reconnection interval (ms) maxReconnectAttempts number 10 Maximum reconnection attempts heartbeatInterval number 30000 Heartbeat ping interval (ms) onOpen function - Called when connection opens onClose function - Called when connection closes onError function - Called on connection error onMessage function - Called on incoming message syncedRune Create a rune that automatically syncs with the server. ; // Local update (optimistic) count.set(5); // Server will be notified // If server rejects, value rolls back`, "typescript", "synced-rune.ts") syncBatch Batch sync multiple runes together for efficient updates. , email: new Rune('') }; // Sync all states under a component ID syncBatch('user-form', states);`, "typescript", "sync-batch.ts") applyStateUpdate Apply server state updates to multiple runes at once. , name: new Rune('') }; // Apply updates from server applyStateUpdate(states, { count: 10, name: 'Updated' });`, "typescript", "apply-state.ts") Connection State Monitor connection state changes. ; // Check current state if (ws.isConnected) { console.log('WebSocket is connected'); } // State values: 'connecting' | 'connected' | 'disconnecting' | 'disconnected' console.log('Current state:', ws.state); // Listen for state changes ws.onStateChange((newState) => { console.log('State changed to:', newState); });`, "typescript", "connection-state.ts") Previous Navigation & Events Next Transitions }

title: Component System
description: The foundation for all GoSPA components, providing core functionality for state management and lifecycle.
content: Component System GoSPA's component system provides a hierarchical structure for managing UI components with lifecycle hooks, props, and state. BaseComponent The foundation for all GoSPA components, providing core functionality for state management and lifecycle. Method Return Description GetID() string Returns unique component identifier GetName() string Returns component name GetState() *StateMap Returns component's state map GetProps() *Props Returns component's props GetChildren() []*BaseComponent Returns child components AddChild(child) error Adds a child component RemoveChild(id) error Removes a child by ID ToJSON() []byte Serializes component to JSON Clone() *BaseComponent Creates a deep copy ComponentTree Manages the hierarchical structure of components with traversal and search capabilities. // Walk the tree tree.Walk(func(c *BaseComponent) bool { fmt.Println(c.GetName()) return true // continue walking }) // Find a component by ID found := tree.Find("component-id") // Find all matching components matches := tree.FindAll(func(c *BaseComponent) bool { return c.GetName() == "Button" })`, "go", "example.go") Method Description Root() Returns the root component Get(id) Get component by ID Add(parentID, child) Add child to parent Remove(id) Remove component and descendants Walk(fn) Depth-first traversal Find(id) Find component by ID FindAll(predicate) Find all matching components Lifecycle Components go through defined phases with hooks for each transition. { fmt.Println("Component mounted:", c.GetID()) }) component.OnUpdate(func(c *BaseComponent, oldProps, newProps *Props) { fmt.Println("Props updated") }) component.OnDestroy(func(c *BaseComponent) { // Cleanup resources })`, "go", "lifecycle.go") Phase Hook Description Created OnCreate Component instance created Mounting OnMount Before DOM insertion Mounted OnMounted After DOM insertion Updating OnUpdate Before props/state change Updated OnUpdated After props/state change Destroying OnDestroy Before removal Destroyed OnDestroyed After removal Props Type-safe property access with validation and default values. props.Set("title", "Hello") props.Set("count", 42) title := props.GetString("title") // "Hello" count := props.GetInt("count") // 42 enabled := props.GetBool("enabled", false) // false (default) // With schema validation schema := component.PropSchema{ "title": {Type: "string", Required: true}, "count": {Type: "int", Default: 0}, } props := component.NewPropsWithSchema(schema)`, "go", "props.go") Method Return Description Get(key) any Get raw value GetString(key) string Get as string GetInt(key) int Get as integer GetBool(key, default) bool Get as boolean with default GetFloat64(key) float64 Get as float GetSlice(key) []any Get as slice Set(key, value) - Set a value Has(key) bool Check if key exists Validate() error Validate against schema BindableProp Two-way binding support for parent-child communication. // Subscribe to changes name.OnChange(func(newVal string) { fmt.Println("Name title: Scaling and Security
description: If Prefork is enabled, you MUST provide external implementations for Storage and PubSub (e.g. Redis), otherwise state and WebSockets will be isolated to individual child processes.
content:
← Configuration Scaling and Security Distributed deployment, horizontal scaling, and security configuration. Distributed and Scaling Options Option Type Default Description Prefork bool false Enables Fiber Prefork Storage store.Storage memory External Key-Value store (e.g. Redis) for Sessions/State PubSub store.PubSub memory External broker (e.g. Redis PubSub) for broadcasts SSGCacheTTL time.Duration 0 TTL for SSG cache (0 = forever) SSGCacheMaxEntries int 500 Max SSG cache entries (FIFO) If Prefork is enabled, you MUST provide external implementations for Storage and PubSub (e.g. Redis), otherwise state and WebSockets will be isolated to individual child processes. Security Options Option Type Default Description AllowedOrigins []string [] Allowed CORS origins EnableCSRF bool false Enable automatic CSRF protection RemoteActionMiddleware fiber.Handler nil Optional global middleware for remote action auth/policy checks Hydration Options Option Type Default Description HydrationMode string "" Hydration strategy: "immediate", "lazy", "visible", "idle" HydrationTimeout int 0 Milliseconds before force hydrate Prefork Best Practices Prefork mode maximizes performance by spawning a worker process { "for" } each CPU core. To ensure consistency across workers, follow these rules: External Storage: You MUST use an external storage backend like Redis { "for" } session and client state. In-memory state is isolated per process. External PubSub: WebSocket broadcasting requires a shared broker (e.g. Redis PubSub) to reach clients connected to different worker processes. Avoid Global Variables: Mutable global variables are the primary source of bugs in Prefork mode. Each process has its own copy. Move shared logic to external databases or coordinated state. Port Sharing: Fiber handles SO_REUSEPORT automatically, but ensure your OS allows multiple processes to bind to the same port. Examples High-Performance (Cluster Mode via Prefork) ,
 PubSub: redis.NewPubSub(rdb),
 SimpleRuntime: true,
 CompressState: true,
 StateDiffing: true,
 CacheTemplates: true,
 HydrationMode: "lazy",
})", "go", "main.go") } if prev != "" { Previous } else { } if next != "" { Next } else { } }

title: Project Structure
description: Anatomy of a GoSPA application.
content: Project Structure Anatomy of a GoSPA application. File Hierarchy A standard GoSPA project follows this structure: ├── lib/ # Shared Go code (optional) ├── static/ # Public assets (images, CSS, JS) ├── generated/ # Auto-generated TypeScript bindings └── gospa.json # Optional project configuration`, "plaintext", "File Structure") The `routes/` Directory Your UI lives here. GoSPA looks for `.templ` files to build its routing table. Supporting nested directories, dynamic parameters like [id].templ , and layout inheritance. Layout Files GoSPA supports two types of layout files: File Purpose Scope root_layout.templ Outer HTML shell ( <html> , <head> , <body> ) Entire application layout.templ Nested layouts for sections Route segment and children root_layout.templ — Place at routes/root_layout.templ to define the outermost HTML document. Must include the GoSPA runtime script. Only one root layout exists per app. layout.templ — Regular layouts that wrap pages. Create in subdirectories (e.g., routes/blog/layout.templ ) to wrap all pages in that section. Multiple nested layouts are supported. Wraps entire app (Requires routing.RegisterRootLayout()) ├── layout.templ # Optional root wrapper ├── page.templ # Home page ├── about/ │ └── page.templ # About page └── dashboard/ ├── layout.templ # Dashboard sidebar/header └── page.templ # Dashboard home`, "plaintext", "Layout Hierarchy Example") The `generated/` Directory GoSPA automatically synchronizes your Go types and routes with TypeScript. This directory contains the generated JS/TS files that power the client-side runtime. Do not edit these files manually. Previous Quick Start Next Routing }

title: Hot Module Replacement
description: GoSPA's HMR system consists of three parts:
content: Hot Module Replacement Update code and styles instantly without losing application state. How HMR Works GoSPA's HMR system consists of three parts: Server Manager : Watches for file changes and broadcasts updates to clients. Client Runtime : Receives updates and applies them to the current page. State Preservation : Saves and restores reactive state across updates. Server-Side (Go) The HMRManager orchestrates the update cycle. Configure and start it in your development entry point. hmr.Start() // Register the WebSocket endpoint app.Get("/__hmr", hmr.HMREndpoint())`, "go", "main.go") Client-Side (TypeScript) The HMRClient manages the WebSocket connection and applies updates. ; hmr.onUpdate((msg) => { console.log('Module updated:', msg.moduleId); });`, "typescript", "app.ts") Module Registration For a module to be hot-swappable, it must be registered: ; acceptHMR('my-module');`, "typescript", "module.ts") State Preservation GoSPA can preserve reactive state (Rune, Derived, etc.) across updates. Automatic: If you use the standard StateMap and components, GoSPA automatically serializes state before an update and restores it after. Manual: Hook into the state preservation cycle: => { return { scrollY: window.scrollY }; }; window.__gospaSetState = (id, state) => { window.scrollTo(0, state.scrollY); };`, "typescript", "app.ts") CSS Hot Reloading When a .css file is modified, the CSSHMR system identifies the corresponding link tag and forces a reload by appending a timestamp, avoiding a full page refresh. ;`, "typescript", "styles.ts") Troubleshooting Full Page Reload : If a module cannot be hot-swapped (e.g., changes to internal Go logic or complex dependency graphs), HMR falls back to a full page reload. Connection Lost : The client will attempt to reconnect with exponential backoff. If the server is restarted, the client refreshes the page to ensure synchronization. Console Logs : Enable ` + "`" + `debug: true` + "`" + ` in HMRClientConfig to see the internal HMR event log in the browser console. }

title: Islands & Streaming
description: Control when your components become interactive using data-gospa-mode :
content: Islands & Streaming Selective hydration and progressive SSR for maximum performance. Ship minimal JavaScript by default and hydrate only interactive components. Hydration Modes Control when your components become interactive using data-gospa-mode : immediate Hydrate as soon as the script loads (default). visible Hydrate when element enters viewport (Intersection Observer). idle Hydrate when browser is idle (requestIdleCallback). interaction Hydrate on first click, focus, or hover. lazy Hydrate only when manually triggered via API. Priority Levels When multiple islands are scheduled, the PriorityScheduler ensures critical components load first. Level Value Use Case critical 100 Navigation, search, purchase buttons. high 75 Above-the-fold interactive elements. normal 50 (Default) General interactive components. low 25 Below-the-fold or non-essential widgets. ; scheduler.forceHydrate('gospa-island-123'); console.log(scheduler.getStats()); // { total: 10, pending: 2, hydrated: 8, ... }`, "typescript", "app.ts") Streaming SSR Send the HTML skeleton immediately and stream in islands and state updates as they resolve. error { return fiber.RenderStream(c, DashboardPage()) })`, "go", "main.go") Streaming Features Progressive Hydration : Islands hydrate as soon as their HTML chunk arrives. Out-of-Order Updates : Stream content to specific placeholders after main page renders. Automatic State Sync : The __GOSPA_STATE__ is updated automatically as chunks arrive. Client-Side Streaming The StreamingManager handles incoming chunks automatically: ; document.addEventListener('gospa:hydrated', (ev) => { console.log('Island hydrated:', ev.detail.island.name); });`, "typescript", "streaming.ts") Chunk Types Type Action html Updates a specific DOM element by ID. island Registers and schedules an island for hydration. script Dynamically injects and executes a script. state Updates the global reactive state. error Reports a server-side rendering error. Island Module Convention Islands should export an object with hydrate or mount functions: { // Initialize your framework (Svelte, Preact, Vanilla) console.log('Hydrating Counter with props:', props); } }`, "typescript", "islands/Counter.ts") By default, the IslandManager expects modules at /islands/{"{name}"}.js . Customize in initIslands() . }

title: Introduction
description: Sub-15KB runtime and optimized SSR ensure your apps load instantly and perform smoothly.
content: Introduction GoSPA is a modern framework for building reactive single-page applications using Go and Templ. It brings the power of Svelte-like reactivity to the Go ecosystem without compromising on performance or type safety. Fast by Default Sub-15KB runtime and optimized SSR ensure your apps load instantly and perform smoothly. Truly Reactive Primitive-based reactivity system that works seamlessly between Go server and browser. Why GoSPA? Building SPAs in Go has traditionally been a choice between high-performance APIs with complex JS frontends, or simple SSR with limited interactivity. GoSPA bridges this gap by providing: Key Features Unified Language : Write your UI logic and state management in Go. Automatic Hydration : Server-rendered templates automatically become interactive on the client. Real-time Synchronization : Built-in WebSocket support for collaborative, multi-user experiences. Zero-Config Routing : SvelteKit-inspired file system based routing. Comparison Unlike HTMX which focus on hypermedia updates, GoSPA provides a full reactive state machine mirroring Svelte's approach, but running on your Go server. Note GoSPA is currently in Alpha. While stable enough for documentation projects (like this one!), APIs may change. feedback and contributions are welcome! Next Installation }

title: Route Parameters
description: Extract and manage route parameters from URLs.
content: Route Parameters GoSPA provides type-safe route parameter extraction and URL building utilities. Params Extract and manage route parameters from URLs. // returns string // Get with default page := params.GetDefault("page", "1") // Get all parameters all := params.All() // returns map[string]string // Check if parameter exists if params.Has("id") { // ... }`, "go", "routing/params.go") QueryParams Handle URL query string parameters. // Get all values for a key (multi-value) tags := query.GetAll("tag") // returns []string // Get typed values page := query.GetInt("page", 1) limit := query.GetInt("limit", 10) active := query.GetBool("active", false) // Set a query parameter query.Set("sort", "created_at") // Add a query parameter (appends to existing) query.Add("filter", "active") // Delete a query parameter query.Del("token") // Encode to string encoded := query.Encode() // "q=test&page=1"`, "go", "routing/params.go") ParamExtractor Extract parameters from route patterns. // Extract from URL params, err := extractor.Extract("/users/123/posts/456") // params = {"id": "123", "postId": "456"} // With wildcard patterns extractor := routing.NewParamExtractor("/files/*path") params, _ := extractor.Extract("/files/docs/readme.md") // params = {"path": "docs/readme.md"} // With optional segments extractor := routing.NewParamExtractor("/search/:query?") params, _ := extractor.Extract("/search") // params = {"query": ""}`, "go", "routing/params.go") PathBuilder Build URLs from route patterns and parameters. // Build path with parameters path, err := builder.Build(map[string]string{ "id": "123", "postId": "456", }) // path = "/users/123/posts/456" // Build with query parameters fullURL := builder.BuildWithQuery( map[string]string{"id": "123"}, map[string]string{"sort": "date", "order": "desc"}, ) // fullURL = "/users/123?sort=date&order=desc" // Validate parameters before building if err := builder.Validate(map[string]string{"id": "123"}); err != nil { // Missing required parameter: postId }`, "go", "routing/params.go") Route Patterns Supported route pattern syntax. Pattern Example Description :param /users/:id Named parameter (required) :param? /search/:query? Optional parameter *param /files/*path Wildcard (catch-all) :param(regex) /users/:id(^[0-9]+$) Parameter with regex constraint Client-Side Navigation Type-safe navigation in TypeScript. ; // Build path from parameters const path = buildPath('/users/:id/posts/:postId', { id: '123', postId: '456' }); // path = '/users/123/posts/456' // Get current route parameters const params = getParams(); console.log(params.id); // "123" // Get query parameters const query = getQueryParams(); console.log(query.sort); // "date"`, "typescript", "client/navigation.ts") }

title: Plugin System
description: Minimal plugin with name, initialization, and dependencies.
content: Plugin System Extend GoSPA with powerful plugins for CSS processing, image optimization, form validation, SEO, authentication, and runtime integration. Overview GoSPA's plugin system allows you to extend the framework with additional functionality. Plugins can hook into the build process, add CLI commands, and provide runtime features like middleware and template functions. Plugin Types Base Plugin Minimal plugin with name, initialization, and dependencies. CLI Plugin Extends base plugin with lifecycle hooks and custom CLI commands. Runtime Plugin Extends base plugin with middleware injection, template functions, and runtime configuration. Built-in Plugins Configuration Configure plugins in your gospa.yaml file: Plugin Hooks Plugins can hook into various stages of the build, development, and runtime lifecycle: Hook Description BeforeGenerate Runs before route generation AfterGenerate Runs after route generation BeforeDev Runs before dev server starts AfterDev Runs after dev server stops BeforeBuild Runs before production build AfterBuild Runs after production build BeforeServe Runs before HTTP server starts AfterServe Runs after HTTP server starts BeforePrune Runs before state pruning/cleanup AfterPrune Runs after state pruning/cleanup OnError Runs when an error occurs App Integration Register plugins with your GoSPA application using the UsePlugin method: { app := gospa.New(config) // Register a single plugin app.UsePlugin(myplugin.New(myplugin.Config{ Option1: "value", })) // Or register multiple plugins at once app.UsePlugins(plugin1, plugin2, plugin3) // Get a registered plugin if p, ok := app.GetPlugin("myplugin"); ok { // Use plugin } // List all registered plugins for _, info := range app.ListPlugins() { println(info.Name, info.Version, info.State) } app.Run(":3000") }`, "go", "main.go") Creating Custom Plugins Create your own plugins by implementing the Plugin interface: *MyPlugin { return &MyPlugin{config: config} } func (p *MyPlugin) Name() string { return "my-plugin" } func (p *MyPlugin) Init() error { return nil } func (p *MyPlugin) Dependencies() []plugin.Dependency { return []plugin.Dependency{ {Type: plugin.DepGo, Name: "github.com/example/pkg", Version: "v1.0.0"}, {Type: plugin.DepBun, Name: "some-npm-package", Version: "^2.0.0"}, } }`, "go", "myplugin.go") CLI Plugin with Hooks OnHook(hook plugin.Hook, ctx map[string]interface{}) error { switch hook { case plugin.BeforeBuild: // Pre-build logic case plugin.AfterBuild: // Post-build logic case plugin.BeforeServe: // Before HTTP server starts if fiberApp, ok := ctx["fiber"].(*fiber.App); ok { // Configure fiber app } case plugin.OnError: if errMsg, ok := ctx["error"].(string); ok { // Handle error } } return nil } func (p *MyPlugin) Commands() []plugin.Command { return []plugin.Command{ { Name: "myplugin:run", Alias: "mr", Description: "Run my plugin", Action: p.runAction, }, } }`, "go", "myplugin_hooks.go") Runtime Plugin with Middlewares Config() plugin.PluginConfig { return plugin.PluginConfig{ Schema: map[string]plugin.FieldSchema{ "apiKey": { Type: "string", Description: "API key for the service", Required: true, }, }, Defaults: map[string]interface{}{ "apiKey": "", }, } } func (p *MyPlugin) Middlewares() []interface{} { return []interface{}{ func(c fiber.Ctx) error { // Custom middleware logic return c.Next() }, } } func (p *MyPlugin) TemplateFuncs() map[string]interface{} { return map[string]interface{}{ "formatDate": func(date interface{}) string { // Custom date formatting return "formatted" }, } }`, "go", "myplugin_runtime.go") Plugin State Management Plugins can be enabled/disabled at runtime: // Disable a plugin plugin.Disable("myplugin") // Check plugin state info, ok := plugin.GetPluginInfo("myplugin") if ok && info.State == plugin.StateEnabled { // Plugin is active }`, "go", "plugin_state.go") External Plugins Load plugins from external GitHub repositories: // Load a plugin from GitHub // Supported formats: // - github.com/owner/repo // - github.com/owner/repo@version // - owner/repo // - owner/repo@version p, err := loader.LoadFromGitHub("github.com/username/gospa-plugin-example") // Or use convenience functions err := plugin.InstallPlugin("username/gospa-plugin-example") err := plugin.UninstallPlugin("username/gospa-plugin-example") // List installed plugins entries, err := plugin.ListInstalledPlugins() // Discover available plugins entries, err := plugin.DiscoverPlugins() // Search plugins results, err := plugin.SearchPlugins("tailwind")`, "go", "external_plugins.go") External plugins are cached in ~/.gospa/plugins/ . CLI Commands Each plugin provides CLI commands for common tasks: gospa add tailwind # Add Tailwind CSS to project # PostCSS gospa postcss:config # Generate PostCSS config (alias: pc) gospa postcss:init # Initialize PostCSS (alias: pi) # Image Optimization gospa image:optimize # Optimize all images (alias: io) gospa image:clean # Clean optimized images (alias: ic) gospa image:sizes # List image sizes (alias: is) # Form Validation gospa validation:generate # Generate validation code (alias: vg) gospa validation:create # Create schema file (alias: vc) gospa validation:list # List all schemas (alias: vl) # SEO gospa seo:generate # Generate sitemap and robots.txt (alias: sg) gospa seo:meta # Generate meta tags (alias: sm) gospa seo:structured # Generate JSON-LD (alias: ss) # Authentication gospa auth:generate # Generate auth handlers (alias: ag) gospa auth:secret # Generate JWT secret (alias: as) gospa auth:otp # Generate TOTP secret (alias: ao) gospa auth:backup # Generate backup codes (alias: ab) gospa auth:verify # Verify OTP code (alias: av) # QR Code (programmatic API only - no CLI commands)`, "bash", "terminal") } { title } { description } }

title: PostCSS Plugin
description: Add PostCSS to your project using the add command:
content: PostCSS Plugin Advanced CSS processing with PostCSS and Tailwind CSS v4 integration. Installation Add PostCSS to your project using the add command: This command installs PostCSS dependencies and creates a starter configuration file. The plugin integrates automatically with the build process through lifecycle hooks. Configuration Configure PostCSS in your gospa.yaml: output: ./static/css/main.css # Output CSS file (default: ./static/css/main.css) watch: true # Watch for changes in dev (default: true) minify: true # Minify in production (default: true) sourceMap: false # Generate source maps (default: false) plugins: # Additional Tailwind plugins typography: true # @tailwindcss/typography forms: true # @tailwindcss/forms aspectRatio: true # @tailwindcss/aspect-ratio autoprefixer: true # Add vendor prefixes cssnano: false # Advanced minification postcssNested: true # Nested CSS support # Critical CSS extraction for performance criticalCSS: enabled: false # Enable critical CSS extraction criticalOutput: ./static/css/critical.css # Inlined in HTML nonCriticalOutput: ./static/css/non-critical.css # Async loaded inlineMaxSize: 14336 # Max bytes for inline CSS (14KB default) dimensions: # Viewport sizes for critical CSS - width: 1300 height: 900 name: desktop - width: 500 height: 900 name: mobile # CSS bundle splitting for multi-page apps bundles: - name: marketing input: ./styles/marketing.css output: ./static/css/marketing.css content: # Content paths for this bundle - ./routes/marketing/**/*.templ criticalCSS: enabled: true criticalOutput: ./static/css/marketing.critical.css nonCriticalOutput: ./static/css/marketing.non-critical.css - name: dashboard input: ./styles/dashboard.css output: ./static/css/dashboard.css content: - ./routes/dashboard/**/*.templ`, "yaml", "gospa.yaml") CLI Commands Command Alias Description gospa add:postcss — Install PostCSS deps and create config gospa postcss:build pb Build CSS for production gospa postcss:watch pw Watch and rebuild CSS on changes gospa postcss:config pc Generate PostCSS configuration file gospa postcss:critical pcr Extract critical CSS for above-the-fold content gospa postcss:bundles pbd Build all CSS bundles Lifecycle Hooks Critical CSS and Async Loading Improve page load performance by extracting critical CSS (above-the-fold) and loading non-critical CSS asynchronously. Critical CSS is inlined directly in the HTML, eliminating render-blocking requests for initial page paint. Setup 1. Enable critical CSS in your configuration: dimensions: # Viewport sizes for critical CSS detection - width: 1300 height: 900 name: desktop - width: 500 height: 900 name: mobile`, "yaml", "gospa.yaml") 2. Extract critical CSS: 3. Import the postcss your layout: 4. Use the helper functions in your templates: --> @templ.Raw(" " + postcss.CriticalCSS("./static/css/critical.css") + " ") @templ.Raw(postcss.AsyncCSS("/static/css/non-critical.css"))`, "templ", "root_layout.templ") How It Works Critical CSS - Above-the-fold styles for initial render, inlined in HTML <head> Non-critical CSS - Below-the-fold styles loaded asynchronously via preload link 14KB limit - Ensures critical CSS fits in a single TCP round-trip (HTTP/2 packet) CSS-safe extraction - Splits at complete CSS rules, never mid-declaration Helper Functions postcss.CriticalCSS(path string) string Reads critical CSS file and returns content for inlining. Returns empty string if file doesn't exist. postcss.AsyncCSS(path string) string Generates HTML for async CSS loading with preload and noscript fallback. postcss.CriticalCSSWithFallback(path, fallback string) string Like CriticalCSS but returns fallback content if file doesn't exist (useful for development). postcss.GenerateCriticalCSSHelper(projectDir, criticalCSSPath string) (string, error) Reads critical CSS relative to projectDir. Returns an error if the file cannot be read — prefer this over CriticalCSS when you need explicit error handling at startup. postcss.GenerateAsyncCSSScript(cssPath string) string Low-level helper that returns the raw preload + noscript HTML string. AsyncCSS wraps this function. Performance tip: The AsyncCSS helper generates <link rel="preload"> with an onload handler that switches to stylesheet once loaded. This prevents render-blocking while ensuring styles apply immediately when available. CSS Bundle Splitting For multi-page applications, split CSS into separate bundles to reduce per-page payload. Each bundle scans only its specified content paths for Tailwind class detection. Build all bundles: Each bundle can have its own critical CSS extraction, allowing you to inline only the styles needed for that section of your app. Available Plugins Note: Container queries and line-clamp are built into Tailwind v4 and don't require separate plugins. Generated Configuration The plugin generates a postcss.config.js file: Usage Example ; } /* Use prose classes from typography plugin */ .article { @apply prose prose-lg dark:prose-invert; } /* Use form classes from forms plugin */ .form-input { @apply form-input rounded-lg border-gray-300; } /* Aspect ratio is built into Tailwind v4 */ .video-container { @apply aspect-video w-full; }`, "css", "styles.css") Dependencies Bun packages (auto-installed): postcss-cli, tailwindcss/postcss, tailwindcss/typography, tailwindcss/forms, tailwindcss/aspect-ratio } { name } { pkg } { description } } { name } { description } }

title: QR Code Plugin
description: Enable the QR Code plugin in your gospa.yaml :
content: QR Code Plugin A pure Go QR code generation plugin for GoSPA applications. Generate QR codes for URLs, OTP/TOTP setup, and any other content with customizable size, colors, and error correction. Features ✓ Pure Go implementation - no external dependencies ✓ Multiple output formats: Image, PNG bytes, Base64, Data URL ✓ Configurable error correction levels (Low, Medium, Quartile, High) ✓ Customizable size and colors ✓ Built-in OTP/TOTP QR code generation for auth flows ✓ Functional options pattern for flexible configuration Installation Enable the QR Code plugin in your gospa.yaml : Basic Usage Generate a Simple QR Code { // Generate a QR code as a data URL (for HTML img src) dataURL, err := qrcode.GenerateDataURL("https://example.com") if err != nil { panic(err) } fmt.Println(dataURL) // Output: data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgo... // Generate PNG bytes pngBytes, err := qrcode.GeneratePNG("https://example.com") if err != nil { panic(err) } // Save to file or send as HTTP response }`, "go", "main.go") Customization Options { // Custom size dataURL, _ := qrcode.GenerateDataURL("https://example.com", qrcode.WithSize(512), ) // Custom error correction level dataURL, _ = qrcode.GenerateDataURL("https://example.com", qrcode.WithLevel(qrcode.LevelHigh), ) // Custom colors dataURL, _ = qrcode.GenerateDataURL("https://example.com", qrcode.WithColors(color.Black, color.White), ) // Custom branded colors (e.g., dark blue on light gray) dataURL, _ = qrcode.GenerateDataURL("https://example.com", qrcode.WithSize(400), qrcode.WithLevel(qrcode.LevelQuartile), qrcode.WithColors( color.RGBA{R: 30, G: 60, B: 114, A: 255}, // Foreground color.RGBA{R: 240, G: 244, B: 248, A: 255}, // Background ), ) }`, "go", "main.go") Error Correction Levels Level Recovery Use Case LevelLow ~7% Clean environments, maximum data capacity LevelMedium ~15% General use (default) LevelQuartile ~25% Printed materials, potential damage LevelHigh ~30% Harsh environments, logos/overlays OTP/TOTP Integration The plugin provides a convenient method for generating QR codes compatible with authenticator apps like Google Authenticator, Authy, and 1Password. { // Build the otpauth:// URL issuer := "MyApp" account := "user@example.com" secret := "JBSWY3DPEHPK3PXP" // Base32 encoded secret otpURL := fmt.Sprintf( "otpauth://totp/%s:%s?secret=%s&issuer=%s&algorithm=SHA1&digits=6&period=30", url.QueryEscape(issuer), url.QueryEscape(account), secret, url.QueryEscape(issuer), ) // Generate QR code for authenticator apps dataURL, err := qrcode.ForOTP(otpURL) if err != nil { panic(err) } // Use in HTML template fmt.Printf(" ", dataURL) }`, "go", "main.go") HTTP Handler Example Serve QR codes directly via HTTP endpoints: { app := fiber.New() // Serve QR code as PNG image app.Get("/qr/:content", func(c *fiber.Ctx) error { content := c.Params("content") pngBytes, err := qrcode.GeneratePNG(content, qrcode.WithSize(256), qrcode.WithLevel(qrcode.LevelMedium), ) if err != nil { return c.Status(500).SendString("Failed to generate QR code") } c.Set("Content-Type", "image/png") c.Set("Cache-Control", "public, max-age=86400") // Cache for 24 hours return c.Send(pngBytes) }) // Serve QR code as data URL (for JSON APIs) app.Get("/api/qr/:content", func(c *fiber.Ctx) error { content := c.Params("content") dataURL, err := qrcode.GenerateDataURL(content) if err != nil { return c.Status(500).JSON(fiber.Map{ "error": "Failed to generate QR code", }) } return c.JSON(fiber.Map{ "data_url": dataURL, }) }) app.Listen(":3000") }`, "go", "main.go") API Reference Package Functions (image.Image, error)", "Generates a QR code as an image.Image.") ([]byte, error)", "Generates a QR code as PNG-encoded bytes.") (string, error)", "Generates a QR code as a base64-encoded PNG string.") (string, error)", "Generates a QR code as a data URL (ready for img src).") (string, error)", "Generates a QR code for OTP/TOTP setup (300px default size).") Options Option", "Sets the QR code size in pixels (default: 256).") Option", "Sets the error correction level (default: LevelMedium).") Option", "Sets the foreground and background colors.") Integration with Auth Plugin The QR Code plugin integrates seamlessly with the Authentication plugin for TOTP setup flows. When users enable two-factor authentication, generate a QR code they can scan with their authenticator app. See the Authentication Plugin documentation for complete TOTP implementation examples. } { signature } { description } }

description: Valibot is significantly smaller than Zod (~12KB) while providing the same validation capabilities. Perfect for client-side bundles.
content: Form Validation Plugin Isomorphic form validation with Valibot (client) and Go validator (server). Generates TypeScript types, Valibot schemas, and Go structs from JSON schemas. Why Valibot? Valibot is significantly smaller than Zod (~12KB) while providing the same validation capabilities. Perfect for client-side bundles. Installation CLI Commands Command Alias Description gospa validation:generate vg Generate types and schemas from JSON schemas gospa validation:create vc Create a new validation schema gospa validation:list vl List all validation schemas Schema Definition Generated Output TypeScript Types Valibot Schema , v.email(), v.minLength(5), v.maxLength(255)), password: v.pipe(v.string(), v.minLength(8), v.maxLength(100)), age: v.optional(v.pipe(v.number(), v.minValue(18), v.maxValue(120))) });`, "typescript", "generated/schemas.ts") Server-side Validation error { return validator.New().Struct(user) }`, "go", "generated/user.go") Dependencies Bun packages: valibot Go packages: github.com/go-playground/validator/v10 } { value } { description } }

title: Advanced Types
content: Advanced Types Specialized reactive types for complex use cases and API reference. Resource Async data fetching with loading/error states. ; // Reactive resource - refetches when userId changes const userResource = resourceReactive( [userId], async () => { const res = await fetch('/api/user/' + userId.get()); return res.json(); } ); // Status console.log(userResource.status); // 'idle' | 'pending' | 'success' | 'error' console.log(userResource.isPending); // boolean console.log(userResource.data); // T | undefined console.log(userResource.error); // Error | undefined // Refetch await userResource.refetch();`, "typescript", "resource.ts") RuneRaw Shallow reactive state - only top-level changes tracked. ; // This does NOT trigger updates person.value.nested.age = 31; // This DOES trigger updates (top-level reassignment) person.value = { ...person.value, name: 'New Name' };`, "typescript", "runeraw.ts") PreEffect Effect that runs before DOM updates. => { // Read scroll position before DOM changes const scrollY = window.scrollY; console.log('Scroll position:', scrollY); });`, "typescript", "preeffect.ts") EffectRoot Non-tracked effect scope with manual cleanup. => { // Effects created here are not auto-tracked const e1 = new Effect(() => { /* ... */ }); const e2 = new Effect(() => { /* ... */ }); // Return cleanup for all return () => { e1.dispose(); e2.dispose(); }; }); // Manual cleanup cleanup();`, "typescript", "effectroot.ts") API Reference Go Rune Methods Method Signature Description Get() T Get current value (thread-safe) Set() func(T) Set new value and notify subscribers Update() func(func(T) T) Update using function Subscribe() func(func(T, T)) func() Subscribe to changes, returns unsubscribe TypeScript Rune Methods Property/Method Type Description value T Get/set value directly get() () ={}> T Get value (tracks dependencies) set() (v: T) ={}> void Set new value update() (fn: (v: T) ={}> T) ={}> void Update using function subscribe() (fn: Subscriber) ={}> Unsubscribe Subscribe to changes Why "Runes"? Inspired by Svelte 5's terminology, Runes in GoSPA represent magical symbols that imbue your data with reactive properties. They are the building blocks of an interactive UI that responds naturally to user input and server events. The name reflects their power to transform ordinary values into reactive state. ← Plain JavaScript Back to Overview → }

title: Go Primitives
content: Go Primitives Server-side reactive primitives that are thread-safe and designed for concurrent access in Go handlers and components. Rune // Get value value := count.Get() // Set value count.Set(5) // Update with function count.Update(func(v int) int { return v + 1 }) // Subscribe to changes unsubscribe := count.Subscribe(func(newValue, oldValue int) { log.Printf("Changed from %d to %d", oldValue, newValue) }) // Unsubscribe when done unsubscribe()`, "go", "rune.go") Derived doubled := gospa.Derived(count, func(v int) int { return v * 2 }) fmt.Println(doubled.Get()) // 10 // Derived from multiple runes a := gospa.NewRune(10) b := gospa.NewRune(20) sum := gospa.Derived2(a, b, func(a, b int) int { return a + b }) fmt.Println(sum.Get()) // 30`, "go", "derived.go") Effect // Create effect that runs on changes effect := gospa.Effect(count, func(v int) { log.Printf("Count is now: %d", v) }) // Effect with cleanup effect := gospa.Effect(count, func(v int) func() { log.Printf("Count changed to: %d", v) return func() { log.Println("Cleanup before next run") } }) // Dispose effect when done effect.Dispose()`, "go", "effect.go") StateMap // Set values states.Set("count", 0) states.Set("name", "GoSPA") // Get values countRune := states.Get("count") if countRune != nil { fmt.Println(countRune.Get()) } // Serialize for client json := states.ToJSON() // Deserialize from client states.FromJSON(jsonString) // Batch updates states.Batch(func(m *gospa.StateMap) { m.Set("a", 1) m.Set("b", 2) })`, "go", "statemap.go") Batch Updates b := gospa.NewRune(2) // Batch multiple updates - subscribers notified once gospa.Batch(func() { a.Set(10) b.Set(20) })`, "go", "batch.go") ← Back to Overview TypeScript Primitives → }

title: JavaScript / TypeScript Primitives
description: Here's a complete counter with derived display and effects:
content: JavaScript / TypeScript Primitives Use GoSPA's reactive primitives with TypeScript or vanilla JavaScript. Toggle between JS and TS examples using the language switcher on each code block. Setup Options TypeScript (ES Modules): Import from @gospa/client package Plain JavaScript: Use the global GoSPA object — no build step required Getting Started Include the GoSPA runtime in your HTML, or import via ES modules: --> `, "html", "setup.html") Rune — Reactive State A Rune is a reactive container for a single value. When the value changes, all subscribers are automatically notified. @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript — uses global GoSPA object const count = new GoSPA.Rune(0) // Read the value console.log(count.get()) // 0 // Update the value count.set(5) // Update based on current value count.update(v => v + 1) // Subscribe to changes const unsubscribe = count.subscribe((value, oldValue) => { console.log('Changed from ' + oldValue + ' to ' + value) }) // Later: stop listening unsubscribe()`, `// TypeScript — ES module imports import { Rune, rune } from '@gospa/client'; // Create const count = new Rune(0); const name = rune('initial'); // factory function // Read console.log(count.value); // 0 console.log(count.get()); // 0 // Write count.value = 1; count.set(2); count.update(n => n + 1); // Subscribe const unsubscribe = count.subscribe((value, oldValue) => { console.log('Changed:', oldValue, '->', value); }); // Cleanup unsubscribe();`, "rune.js/ts") Derived — Computed Values Derived creates reactive values that automatically update when their dependencies change. @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript const firstName = new GoSPA.Rune('John') const lastName = new GoSPA.Rune('Doe') // Derived automatically recalculates const fullName = new GoSPA.Derived(() => { return firstName.get() + ' ' + lastName.get() }) console.log(fullName.get()) // "John Doe" firstName.set('Jane') console.log(fullName.get()) // "Jane Doe" — automatically updated`, `// TypeScript import { Rune, Derived, derived } from '@gospa/client'; const count = new Rune(5); // Create derived const doubled = new Derived(() => count.get() * 2); const summary = derived(() => 'Count: ' + count.get()); // Read console.log(doubled.value); // 10 // Subscribe doubled.subscribe(v => console.log('Doubled:', v)); // Cleanup doubled.dispose();`, "derived.js/ts") Effect — Side Effects Effect runs side effects when reactive dependencies change. Return a cleanup function if needed. @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript const count = new GoSPA.Rune(0) const cleanup = new GoSPA.Effect(() => { console.log('Count is now:', count.get()) // Optional cleanup return () => { console.log('Cleaning up previous effect') } }) // Stop the effect cleanup()`, `// TypeScript import { Rune, Effect, effect } from '@gospa/client'; const count = new Rune(0); // Create effect const myEffect = new Effect(() => { console.log('Count changed:', count.get()); // Optional cleanup return () => { console.log('Cleanup before next run'); }; }); // Control myEffect.pause(); // Stop reacting myEffect.resume(); // Resume myEffect.dispose(); // Permanent cleanup`, "effect.js/ts") StateMap — Named State StateMap is a collection of named reactive values, useful for organizing component state. @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript const state = new GoSPA.StateMap() // Set values state.set('count', 0) state.set('name', 'Anonymous') // Get rune objects const countRune = state.get('count') console.log(countRune.get()) // 0 // Check existence console.log(state.has('count')) // true // Delete state.delete('count') // Clear all state.clear() // Serialize const json = state.toJSON() // { count: 0, name: 'Anonymous' }`, `// TypeScript import { StateMap, Rune } from '@gospa/client'; const state = new StateMap(); // Set values with type safety state.set('count', new Rune(0)); state.set('name', new Rune('Anonymous')); // Get typed rune objects const countRune = state.get('count'); // Rune | undefined console.log(countRune?.get()); // 0 // Check existence console.log(state.has('count')); // true // Delete state.delete('count'); // Clear all state.clear(); // Serialize const json = state.toJSON(); // { count: 0, name: 'Anonymous' }`, "statemap.js/ts") Batch Updates Batch multiple updates to prevent unnecessary recomputations: @components.DualCodeBlock( `// Plain JavaScript const count = new GoSPA.Rune(0) const name = new GoSPA.Rune('') GoSPA.batch(() => { count.set(10) name.set('Alice') // Subscribers notified once after batch completes })`, `// TypeScript import { Rune, batch, untrack, watch } from '@gospa/client'; const count = new Rune(0); const name = new Rune(''); // Batch updates - single notification batch(() => { count.set(10); name.set('Alice'); }); // Watch multiple sources const unsub = watch([count, name], (values, oldValues) => { console.log('Changed:', values, oldValues); }); // Untrack - don't track dependency inside effect effect(() => { console.log('Count:', count.get()); untrack(() => { console.log('Untracked:', name.get()); // Not a dependency }); });`, "batch.js/ts") Complete Example Here's a complete counter with derived display and effects: @components.DualCodeBlock( ` Count: 0 Doubled: 0 Status: Even + - // State const count = new GoSPA.Rune(0) // Derived values const doubled = new GoSPA.Derived(() => count.get() * 2) const status = new GoSPA.Derived(() => count.get() % 2 === 0 ? 'Even' : 'Odd' ) // DOM elements const valueEl = document.getElementById('value') const doubledEl = document.getElementById('doubled') const statusEl = document.getElementById('status') // Effects to update DOM new GoSPA.Effect(() => { valueEl.textContent = count.get() }) new GoSPA.Effect(() => { doubledEl.textContent = doubled.get() }) new GoSPA.Effect(() => { statusEl.textContent = status.get() }) // Event listeners document.getElementById('increment').addEventListener('click', () => { count.update(v => v + 1) }) document.getElementById('decrement').addEventListener('click', () => { count.update(v => v - 1) }) `, ` Count: 0 Doubled: 0 Status: Even + - import { Rune, Derived, Effect } from '@gospa/client'; // State const count = new Rune(0); // Derived values const doubled = new Derived(() => count.get() * 2); const status = new Derived(() => count.get() % 2 === 0 ? 'Even' : 'Odd' ); // DOM elements const valueEl = document.getElementById('value')!; const doubledEl = document.getElementById('doubled')!; const statusEl = document.getElementById('status')!; // Effects to update DOM new Effect(() => { valueEl.textContent = String(count.get()); }); new Effect(() => { doubledEl.textContent = String(doubled.get()); }); new Effect(() => { statusEl.textContent = status.get(); }); // Event listeners document.getElementById('increment')!.addEventListener('click', () => { count.update(v => v + 1); }); document.getElementById('decrement')!.addEventListener('click', () => { count.update(v => v - 1); }); `, "counter.html") ← Go Primitives Advanced Types → }

title: Reactive Primitives
description: Reactive state container that notifies subscribers on change.
content: Reactive Primitives GoSPA provides reactive primitives for both server-side Go and client-side TypeScript. These primitives form the foundation of the framework's reactivity system. Core Primitives The reactivity system consists of three core primitives: Rune { "for" } state, Derived { "for" } computed values, and Effect { "for" } side effects. Rune Reactive state container that notifies subscribers on change. Derived Computed values that auto-update when dependencies change. Effect Side effects that run in response to state changes. Topics Go Primitives Thread-safe server-side primitives: Rune, Derived, Effect, and StateMap. TypeScript Primitives Client-side primitives that mirror the server API with DOM integration. Plain JavaScript Pure JavaScript reactive primitives without TypeScript or framework dependencies. Advanced Types Resource, RuneRaw, PreEffect, EffectRoot, and complete API reference. }

title: Remote Actions
content: Remote Actions Type-safe server functions called directly from your templates or client-side scripts. Defining Actions Complex Inputs Calling from Template Calling from Script CSRF Protection Global Guards Defining Actions Remote actions are registered globally using the routing package. They receive a context and an interface which contains the parsed JSON input from the client. { routing.RegisterRemoteAction("greet", func(ctx context.Context, rc routing.RemoteContext, input any) (any, error) { name := input.(string) return "Hello, " + name, nil }) }`, "go", "actions.go") Complex Inputs & Validation Since inputs are passed as any , you should use type assertions or a library like mitchellh/mapstructure to map to your structs. (any, error) { var data CreateUser // Map input to struct... if data.Username == "" { return nil, errors.New("username required") } return "User " + data.Username + " created", nil })`, "go", "user.go") Calling from Templates Trigger remote actions via JavaScript event handlers using the global GoSPA.remote() function. " class="px-4 py-2 bg-blue-600 rounded" > Greet Me async function handleGreet() { const result = await GoSPA.remote('greet', 'GoSPA User'); if (result.ok) { console.log(result.data); } } `, "templ", "page.templ") @components.Callout(components.CalloutWarning, "Important: GoSPA Initialization", warningContent()) Calling from Script Call remote actions using the GoSPA.remote() function from the runtime. JavaScript (ES Modules) async function handleSignup() { const result = await GoSPA.remote('createUser', { username: 'ayden', email: 'ayden@example.com' }); if (result.error) { alert('Error: ' + result.error); } else { console.log('Success:', result.data); } } // Using the typed remoteAction helper const createUser = GoSPA.remoteAction('createUser'); const result = await createUser({ username: 'ayden', email: 'ayden@example.com' });`, "javascript", "signup.js") TypeScript (with imports) { const result = await remote('createUser', { username: 'ayden', email: 'ayden@example.com' }); if (result.error) { alert('Error: ' + result.error); } else { console.log('Success:', result.data); } } // Using the typed remoteAction helper const createUser = remoteAction ('createUser'); const result = await createUser({ username: 'ayden', email: 'ayden@example.com' });`, "typescript", "signup.ts") CSRF Protection When EnableCSRF is true, GoSPA wires CSRF middleware automatically in the default app setup. `, "go", "main.go") The client reads the csrf_token cookie and sends X-CSRF-Token automatically with GoSPA.remote() requests. Global Guards Use RemoteActionMiddleware to enforce auth/policy checks before every remote action. error { if c.Locals("user") == nil { return c.Status(fiber.StatusUnauthorized).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "unauthorized"}) } return c.Next() }, })`, "go", "main.go") Security Note Remote actions are public endpoints. Always perform authentication and authorization checks inside your action functions using the provided context.Context . Extract session information from the context and validate permissions before executing sensitive operations. } Before using GoSPA.remote() , ensure the GoSPA runtime is initialized. Add data-gospa-auto to your HTML tag for auto-initialization, or call init() manually. See the Troubleshooting page for details. }

title: Rendering Strategies
description: Register strategy directly in your route's init() function:
content: Rendering Strategies GoSPA supports four per-page rendering strategies. Mix them freely across routes to get the right performance and freshness trade-off for each page. Strategy Overview Strategy When to Use Cache-Control StrategySSR Auth-gated pages, real-time data, per-user content (default) no-store StrategySSG Fully static: marketing pages, landing pages, docs public, max-age=31536000, immutable StrategyISR Mostly static, acceptable to serve stale for N minutes public, s-maxage=<TTL>, stale-while-revalidate StrategyPPR Static shell (nav, footer) + dynamic inner sections per-request no-store All strategies require CacheTemplates: true in your app config — except SSR, which always renders fresh. SSR — Server-Side Rendering Every request triggers a fresh render. This is the default and requires no configuration. // Or explicitly: routing.RegisterPageWithOptions("/dashboard", dashboardPage, routing.RouteOptions{ Strategy: routing.StrategySSR, })`, "go", "routes.go") SSG — Static Site Generation The page is rendered once on first request and cached until SSGCacheTTL expires or it is evicted by FIFO policy or server restart. Subsequent requests are served instantly from the in-memory cache. ⚠️ Cache Forever Warning If SSGCacheTTL is set to 0 (default), SSG entries do not automatically expire. If you use SSG for content that changes over time (like user profiles or dashboards), your server memory will grow unbounded. We highly recommend adding a TTL or using ISR instead for dynamic pathways. // Enable caching in config app := gospa.New(gospa.Config{ CacheTemplates: true, SSGCacheMaxEntries: 500, // default for in-memory SSGCacheTTL: 1 * time.Hour, // optional cache expiration // Optional: Configure an external store like Redis to share cache across processes Storage: redisstore.NewStore(redisClient), })`, "go", "main.go") ISR — Incremental Static Regeneration ISR extends SSG with a TTL . The page is cached on first request. When a request arrives after the TTL expires, the stale cached version is returned immediately and a background goroutine re-renders and updates the cache — the stale-while-revalidate pattern. // Set a global ISR TTL fallback in config app := gospa.New(gospa.Config{ CacheTemplates: true, DefaultRevalidateAfter: 10 * time.Minute, })`, "go", "routes.go") ISR Behaviour Scenario Result Cache miss (first request) Render synchronously, store, respond Cache hit, age < TTL Serve from cache immediately (fresh) Cache hit, age >= TTL Serve stale cache immediately; background goroutine re-renders Multiple simultaneous stale requests Only one goroutine launched (deduplicated) Interaction with Prefork In Prefork mode ( Prefork: true ), if no external `Storage` is configured, each child process has its own independent in-memory cache. Cache entries are not shared between processes. ISR TTLs fire independently per process. To support shared SSG, ISR, and PPR caching across Prefork child processes or horizontally scaled clusters, configure the Storage option in gospa.Config with a distributed backend such as Redis. This ensures consistent cache hits and dedicates a single background revalidation per route across the entire cluster. PPR — Partial Prerendering PPR renders a static shell of the page (header, nav, footer, layout skeleton) once and caches it. Per-request, only the named dynamic slots are re-rendered and merged into the shell before responding. This gives you SSG speed for the page frame and SSR freshness for dynamic content. Step 1 — Register the page routing.RegisterSlot("/dashboard", "feed", feedSlot) routing.RegisterSlot("/dashboard", "notifications", notificationsSlot)`, "go", "routes.go") Step 2 — Use DynamicSlot in your component ) @gospatmpl.DynamicSlot("notifications", NotificationsComponent(props)) ... // ← baked into cached shell }`, "go", "page.templ") During the shell-build pass, DynamicSlot emits a <!--gospa-slot:name--> placeholder. On each live request the framework replaces each placeholder with the freshly rendered slot wrapped in <div data-gospa-slot="name"> . Per-Page Strategy Selector Option A — Inline init() Register strategy directly in your route's init() function: func init() { routing.RegisterPageWithOptions("/blog", blogPage, routing.RouteOptions{ Strategy: routing.StrategyISR, RevalidateAfter: 5 * time.Minute, }) }`, "go", "page.go") Option B — page.options.go (code generator) Place a companion file next to your page.templ : Global Strategy Defaults Set defaults in gospa.Config { "for" } pages that do not specify their own strategy: `, "go", "main.go") Quick Reference routing.RegisterPage("/", indexPage) // SSG routing.RegisterPageWithOptions("/about", aboutPage, routing.RouteOptions{ Strategy: routing.StrategySSG, }) // ISR — revalidate every 5 minutes routing.RegisterPageWithOptions("/blog", blogPage, routing.RouteOptions{ Strategy: routing.StrategyISR, RevalidateAfter: 5 * time.Minute, }) // PPR — static shell + per-request slots routing.RegisterPageWithOptions("/dashboard", dashboardPage, routing.RouteOptions{ Strategy: routing.StrategyPPR, DynamicSlots: []string{"feed", "notifications"}, }) routing.RegisterSlot("/dashboard", "feed", feedSlot) routing.RegisterSlot("/dashboard", "notifications", notificationsSlot)`, "go", "routes.go") }
